concerns.text: FR-008 mandates a 'balanced dataset' (50/50 clean vs. injected) for the bug detection task. Tasks T022 and T026 implement mutation injection and logging but lack a specific task to sample, filter, or balance the dataset to meet the 50/50 requirement. Without this, the experimental control condition defined in FR-008 is not preserved.

FR-007 requires a 'Tokenization Impact Analysis' that flags results if token count changes exceed a threshold T. Tasks T028 and T040 implement counting and correlation but omit the task to define, determine, or hardcode the threshold T. This makes the requirement untestable and the flagging logic impossible to execute.

FR-005 mandates a normality check (Shapiro-Wilk) followed by a 'robust alternative (e.g., non-parametric permutation test)' if residuals are non-normal. Tasks T034 and T035 only implement the Shapiro-Wilk check and the fitting of LMM/GLMM models. There is no task to implement the non-parametric fallback or the logic to switch models based on the normality result, violating the requirement's conditional logic.

FR-004b specifies that the 'buggy' label is true only if a mutation was applied AND the sample is part of the 'injected half'. Tasks T022 and T026 focus on generating variants and logging types but lack a task to explicitly construct the 'injected half' subset or define the logic mapping variants to the specific ground truth labels required for Precision/Recall/F1 calculation.

SC-001 requires measuring and recording the 'proportion of successfully generated and syntactically valid style variants'. Tasks T008 and T012 implement the validation and generation but lack a specific task to aggregate these results, calculate the success proportion, and record it as a metric, leaving the success criterion unmeasured.
